'''Tyrone McCarthy''' (born 21 April 1988) is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]]. He is the head coach of [[Western Suburbs Magpies]]

He previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] and the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the Super League. McCarthy played for the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] in the [[2014 Queensland Cup|Queensland Cup]], before returning to the top flight in Europe with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. He played for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[NRL]], spending time with their feeder club [[Illawarra Cutters|Illawarra]] in the [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/> He then spent four seasons at the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League|European Super League]].

==Background==
McCarthy was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire]], England, with his family hailing from [[Clonality]], County Cork, Ireland.

==Club career==
===Warrington Wolves===
McCarthy made his début in the [[Super League]] for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on 14 August 2009 against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. He played as a substitute in the [[2009 Challenge Cup Final]] in only his third first-team appearance.<ref name="Surreal experience for rising Warrington Wolves star Tyrone McCarthy">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/4556635.Surreal_experience_for_rising_Wolves_star_Tyrone_McCarthy|title=Surreal experience for rising Warrington Wolves star Tyrone McCarthy|publisher=warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

McCarthy spent time on loan in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] with [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in 2010 and at fellow Super League club the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in 2011, making 7 appearances before returning to his parent club.

He scored a try in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

===Northern Pride===
In 2014 he signed for the [[Queensland Cup]] side [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]], playing a key role as the Pride won the [[2014 Queensland Cup|2014 Intrust Super Cup]], and the inaugural [[NRL State Championship]].

===Hull KR===
McCarthy returned to the Super League in 2015, signing with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. Following a season-ending injury to captain [[Terry Campese]], McCarthy was named as the acting captain for the remainder of the year and led the team out at [[Wembley Stadium]] for the [[2015 Challenge Cup]] Final, which Hull Kingston Rovers lost 50–0 to Leeds.<ref name="Tyrone McCarthy leads Hull KR against Leeds in Challenge Cup final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/aug/28/tyrone-mccarthy-hull-kr-leeds-challenge-cup-final|title=Tyrone McCarthy leads Hull KR against Leeds in Challenge Cup final|publisher=theguardian.com|date=28 August 2015|access-date=1 January 2016}}</ref>

===St. George Illawarra===
[[File:Tryone McCarthy.jpg|thumb|Tryone McCarthy lining up for Ireland]]
On 7 September 2015 it was announced that Tyrone McCarthy signed for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] on a two-year deal beginning in 2016.<ref>{{cite news 
| title = Tyrone McCarthy: Hull KR captain to join St George-Illawarra in NRL
| publisher = BBC Sport
| date = 2015-09-07
| url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/34170647
| access-date = 2015-09-10}}</ref>

In September 2016, McCarthy was named as a {{rlp|sr}} in the 2016 [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|author=NSWRL.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/2016-intrust-super-cup-team-of-the-year-23923|title=2016 Intrust Super Cup Team of the Year|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2016-03-26|access-date=2016-09-23}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

He played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat for Salford against Leeds at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
On 6 February 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=6 Feb 2021|accessdate=7 Feb 2021|title=Tyrone McCarthy: Leigh Centurions sign Ireland back-rower after Salford exit|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55963326}}</ref>

==International career==
McCarthy is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international, having made his début in a victory over Serbia in 2009. He was named Ireland vice-captain in the build-up to the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Finn named Ireland captain for World Cup">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/finn-named-ireland-captain-for-world-cup/tabid/10874/newsid/70403/default.aspx|title=Finn named Ireland captain for World Cup|publisher=nrl.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
On 16 October 2025 he was announced as the new head-coach of Australian NSW Cup side [[Western Suburbs Magpies]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Warrington Guardian|date=16 Oct 2025|author=Matt Turner|title=Tyrone McCarthy named as Wests Tigers' NSW Cup head coach|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/25547937.tyrone-mccarthy-named-wests-tigers-nsw-cup-head-coach/}}</ref>
